title: T-005 — Migration: lab_results and supplier_specs tables
prompt:
# T-005 — Migration: lab_results and supplier_specs tables

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §5.5, §10.6
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Lab results + supplier specs tables with RLS and result-status enum.

ATP swab threshold default 10 RLU (V-TEC-44 trigger lives in T-026, not here). work_order_id FK lives in 08-PRODUCTION; here it is a soft UUID column. declared_allergens stored as TEXT[] (Postgres array) — supplier-declared codes resolved against allergens_reference at API layer.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- db/migrations/0xxx_lab_supplier.sql [create]
- db/schema/lab.ts [create]
- db/schema/supplier-specs.ts [create]
- tests/db/lab-supplier.migration.test.ts [create]

## Out of scope
- ATP threshold trigger (T-026)
- Lab UI (T-049)
- Supplier portal API (Phase 2)

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration applied, when inserting lab_result with invalid test_type, then CHECK rejects.
2. Given supplier_specs row, when reading declared_allergens, then it returns TEXT[] array.
3. Given RLS active, when no org context, then both tables return 0 rows.

## Test strategy
- RED test for CHECK + RLS + array column.
- Run: pnpm vitest run tests/db/lab-supplier.migration.test.ts

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement ATP auto-fail trigger here — it is its own task (T-026).
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
